title: Scope, linkage and storage duration

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Scope, linkage and storage duration; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ISO C Working Group C23 status; ISO/IEC 9899:2024; provisions applicable to Scope, linkage and storage duration
sources.claim: Scope controls name visibility, linkage connects declarations across translation units, and storage duration controls object lifetime independently of those name rules.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Scope controls name visibility, linkage connects declarations across translation units, and storage duration controls object lifetime independently of those name rules.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A block automatic counter is created on each call, while a block static counter retains its value across calls but remains named only inside the function. Your goal is to resolve name visibility and lifetime in a multi-function example. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: while, do and for; pf abstraction calls; Declarations, prototypes and definitions. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: Scope controls name visibility, linkage connects declarations across translation units, and storage duration controls object lifetime independently of those name rules. This rule is applied to concrete C source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Block-scope static objects retain state, extern declarations do not allocate a new definition, and thread storage or tentative definitions add further cases. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: A static local variable is globally visible because it lives for the entire program.

includes: Distinguish block/file scope, internal/external linkage and automatic/static duration.
Resolve name visibility and lifetime in a multi-function example.
excludes: Block-scope static objects retain state, extern declarations do not allocate a new definition, and thread storage or tentative definitions add further cases.

outcomes: Resolve name visibility and lifetime in a multi-function example.

examples.title: Scope, linkage and storage duration: defined case
examples.situation: A block automatic counter is created on each call, while a block static counter retains its value across calls but remains named only inside the function.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A block automatic counter is created on each call, while a block static counter retains its value across calls but remains named only inside the function.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.
examples.result: The defined result follows because A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.

examples.title: Scope, linkage and storage duration: boundary case
examples.situation: A file-scope static helper has internal linkage and cannot be named as the same entity from another translation unit.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A file-scope static helper has internal linkage and cannot be named as the same entity from another translation unit.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Block-scope static objects retain state, extern declarations do not allocate a new definition, and thread storage or tentative definitions add further cases.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: A static local variable is globally visible because it lives for the entire program.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Resolve name visibility and lifetime in a multi-function example.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A static local variable is globally visible because it lives for the entire program.
misconceptions.correction: A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete C rules, explain why this belief fails: A static local variable is globally visible because it lives for the entire program.

analogy.example: A manually managed workshop can model Scope, linkage and storage duration: every tool, address, buffer, and acquired resource has an explicit valid range and owner.
usefulFor: Making low-level state, bounds, representation, ownership, and cleanup responsibilities concrete
limitations: C abstract-machine rules, undefined behavior, pointer provenance, translation, and implementation variation are stricter than a physical workshop analogy.
Block-scope static objects retain state, extern declarations do not allocate a new definition, and thread storage or tentative definitions add further cases.
